issue.title: Animate dep graph edges only when work is actively flowing through them

successCriteria.description: An edge is animated iff its source node is completed/closed AND its target node is executing
All other edges (both endpoints pending, all completed, failed targets, etc.) remain static (animated: false)
Critical Path mode's animation behavior is preserved (its styledEdges transform overlays animation on critical edges)
Hover lineage from #149 still works — animation is orthogonal to dimming/highlighting

phases.title: Set animated flag from source/target status pairing
verification: Read post-edit useDependencyGraph.ts: confirm statusByNodeId map exists and edges' animated flag is computed from source+target status
Eyeball the v0.5.2 graph in the desktop app: should be all-pending currently (no executing issues), so NO edge animation — confirms the negative case
If feasible, manually trigger an executing state on an issue with a completed upstream to see animation; otherwise rely on logic review (the rule is small enough)
Toggle Critical Path mode — critical edges still animate (no regression on the override path)
Hover any node — lineage still dims/highlights correctly (no regression)
